_comment: Prompt Ledger. Store or link major reusable prompts. Use 'body' for an inline copyable prompt, or 'link' for a path/URL. Keep bodies short; link out to docs for long ones.

items.title: AI Command Center v1 kickoff (placeholder — next clean PR)
items.purpose: The next build lane after Mission Control v1. Scope to be defined by the owner; this is a placeholder, not a finished spec.
items.body: PLACEHOLDER — owner to define scope. Build AI Command Center v1 as its own clean PR (one lane), after the merged Mission Control v1 static foundation and before the D1/R2 operations database + document vault. Keep honest labels; no faked live control; no secrets committed. Standing up any backend (Worker/D1/R2/API keys) is an owner-only infra decision requiring explicit OWNER-OK.

items.title: Mission Control v1 kickoff
items.purpose: The prompt that created this dashboard — reuse to extend or rebuild Mission Control.
items.body: Build a gated /lab/mission-control operations dashboard for Devbiz. Sections: Owner Action Queue (first), Active Project Lanes, Active PR Board, Agent Registry (reported status only), Decision Log, Prompt Ledger, Agent Reporting Protocol. Render from data/*.json so future agents update the registry, not the UI. Keep zero-build/static, dark-theme, mobile-usable, honest labels (reported/manual/unknown). One PR lane only.

items.title: Aether Wing Phase 1 kickoff
items.purpose: Start the first playable slice once the frontier-al folder/repo is confirmed.
items.body: PRECONDITION: owner has confirmed the frontier-al folder/repo. Build Aether Wing Phase 1 as a playable slice in the frontier-al Vite/TypeScript/Three.js scaffold (NOT in Devbiz). No wallet/blockchain/account systems. Deliver: one PR lane, a verifiable slice with active-play screenshots, and a closeout (Asked/Done/Needs you). Devbiz only keeps the gated placeholder route.

items.title: Agent Reporting Template
items.purpose: The required reporting block every future agent ends with. Full copyable version is in the Agent Reporting Protocol section and in lab/mission-control/README.md.
items.body:
Asked:
Done:
Needs you:

Repo:
Branch:
PR:
CI status:
Preview/dev route:
Files changed:
Verification performed:
Owner action now:
Next recommended unit:
Agent state: active / parked / subscribed / complete / unknown

Rules: never say "standing by" when owner action is required; say "Owner action now:" when the owner must click/test/approve/merge/configure; say "No owner action right now" when nothing is needed; always report agent state.

items.title: Environment setup
items.purpose: How to set local env values without committing secrets.
items.body: Copy .env.example to .env.local and fill in private values locally. Never commit real secrets. .env, .env.local, .env.*.local are gitignored. .env.example / .env.local.example hold placeholder keys only — no real values. Mission Control itself needs NO secrets (static, client-side); this template exists for future lanes that do.

items.title: Future lane kickoff rule
items.purpose: The standing rule before any new lane begins.
items.body: First update Mission Control with this lane (add it to data/projects.json + data/agents.json, and any owner action to data/actions.json), THEN proceed. No new lane starts unregistered. Report into Mission Control using the Agent Reporting Template on completion.
